name: Chernobog (Slavic mythology)
description:
Chernobog is the god of cold, death, evil and madness. According to legend, he lived in the underworld (Navi) and could not leave it, since the gates of Navi were sealed by Rod, 💀💀💀💀💀💀💀Svarog and Belobog.💀💀💀💀💀💀

According to one hypothesis, the Slavs worshiped Chernobog so that he (the lord of everything bad and dark) would not send any troubles and misfortunes on them either in the afterlife or in this life. He was depicted as an old man with a black beard.

In all legends, songs and tales, he appears as a kind of black entity, the enemy of the light gods and the ruler of all the armies of the evil afterlife, the underworld, hell, the hellish world. In addition, Chernobog is called the Black Serpent and all his sons, daughters, and descendants are called Zmeeviches or Zmieviches. So Viy is called Viy 💀💀💀💀💀💀Zmievich.💀💀💀💀💀💀💀💀

you ask in which religions does it appear?

Chernobog is a character in Slavic mythology, in particular the mythology of the Baltic Slavs.

According to Helmold's "Slavic Chronicle", Chernobog is an evil god who brings misfortune.

Chernobog is also mentioned in the mythology of the Balts, where he is called Vielona, Velns or Vels and is described as a constant enemy of the Thunderer and the master of the world of the dead.

It should be noted that the historicity and divine status of Chernobog are controversial, and there is no consensus among scholars about him.
